title: Virtual Memory
summary: Explains virtual memory (swap): swapping pages to disk, the page file, demand paging, replacement policies, and the dirty bit optimisation.
content_points: Virtual memory: use disk as an extension of RAM; inactive pages saved to page file.
Demand paging: load a page only when accessed (page fault trap triggers OS to load it).
Working set: the subset of pages actually needed at a given time; if it fits in RAM, disk access is rare.
Replacement policy: evict the page least likely to be needed soon (LRU approximation).
Dirty bit: per-page flag set on first write; on eviction, only dirty pages need to be written to disk.
Page fault flow: trap → OS finds page in page file → load into free frame → update page table → retry instruction.
learning_objectives: Explain virtual memory and demand paging
Describe the page fault handling sequence
Discuss replacement policies for virtual memory
